hypothesis: If LLM agents using the ReACT architecture and the provided experiment-submission and sensor-query tools are given greater model capability (e.g., gpt-5 versus smaller variants), larger experimental budgets (more allowed experiments and/or longer reactor runtime), and access to QCM reference measurements, then they will more reliably infer the true rejection rule in the Alien Market and the underlying reaction and transport mechanisms in the ALP reactor (as measured by manual scoring and UMAP proximity to expert-labeled reference experiments), because increased reasoning capacity, exploration opportunities, and richer sensor information enable better hypothesis generation and experimental design from sparse pressure and QCM data.
